title: ammodenia peploides subsp. maritima
intro: Ammodenia peploides subsp. maritima, also known as saltwort, is a succulent plant species that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Ammodenia peploides subsp. maritima include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, showcasing its versatility in traditional medicine.
